---
title: "About"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="masthead">
  <span>Electoral Atlas · Spanish Second Republic · 1931–1936</span>
  <div class="lang-switcher">
    <a href="../en/about.html" class="lang-btn active">EN</a>
    <a href="../es/about.html" class="lang-btn">ES</a>
    <a href="../ca/about.html" class="lang-btn">CA</a>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="page-header">
  <div class="page-header-flag"></div>
  <div class="page-header-label">The team</div>
  <h1 class="page-header-title">About the Project</h1>
  <p class="page-header-sub">Who we are and what we are trying to understand</p>
</div>

<section class="content-section">

  <!-- PROJECT DESCRIPTION -->
  <div class="section-label">The research</div>
  <h2 class="section-title">Why map the Second Republic's elections?</h2>
  <p class="section-body">
    The Spanish Second Republic (1931–1939) held three parliamentary elections under universal
    suffrage — including the first election in Spain in which women could vote (1933). These
    elections took place in a highly polarised context and their results have been the subject
    of intense historical and political debate ever since.
  </p>
  <p class="section-body">
    Yet comprehensive, systematic, <em>municipal-level</em> data for all three elections has
    never been assembled in a single, consistent, openly accessible dataset. This project
    fills that gap. By recovering and digitising results from provincial archives, the INE,
    and regional records, we aim to provide a resource for historians, political scientists,
    and the public alike.
  </p>

  <div class="pull-quote">
    <p>"The geography of the Republic's elections encodes the geography of the Civil War
    that followed. To understand one, you must understand the other."</p>
    <cite>Project description, 2024</cite>
  </div>

  <!-- TEAM -->
  <div class="section-label mt-lg">The team</div>
  <h3 class="section-title" style="font-size:1.3rem;">Who we are</h3>
  <div class="team-grid">

    <div class="team-card">
      <!-- Replace initials div with <img src="..." class="logo-img"> when you have a photo -->
      <div class="team-avatar">YN</div>
      <div class="team-name">Your Name</div>
      <div class="team-role">Principal Investigator</div>
      <p class="team-bio">
        Associate Professor in the Department of Political and Social Sciences at
        Universitat Pompeu Fabra. Research interests: electoral behaviour, historical
        political economy, Southern European politics.
      </p>
      <div class="team-links">
        <a href="#" class="team-link">UPF profile</a>
        <a href="#" class="team-link">Google Scholar</a>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="team-card">
      <div class="team-avatar">RA</div>
      <div class="team-name">Research Assistant</div>
      <div class="team-role">Data Collection &amp; Coding</div>
      <p class="team-bio">
        PhD candidate at Universitat Pompeu Fabra. Responsible for primary source
        collection, data coding, and quality control across all three election years.
      </p>
      <div class="team-links">
        <a href="#" class="team-link">UPF profile</a>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>

  <!-- FUNDING -->
  <div class="section-label mt-lg">Funding &amp; acknowledgements</div>
  <div class="info-grid">
    <div class="info-item">
      <div class="info-item-label">Funded by</div>
      <div class="info-item-value">Ministerio de Ciencia e Innovación</div>
    </div>
    <div class="info-item">
      <div class="info-item-label">Grant reference</div>
      <div class="info-item-value">PID2023-XXXXXX</div>
    </div>
    <div class="info-item">
      <div class="info-item-label">Host institution</div>
      <div class="info-item-value">Universitat Pompeu Fabra</div>
    </div>
    <div class="info-item">
      <div class="info-item-label">Period</div>
      <div class="info-item-value">2023–2026</div>
    </div>
  </div>

</section>

<footer class="site-footer">
  <p>Electoral Atlas of the Spanish Second Republic &nbsp;·&nbsp; Universitat Pompeu Fabra &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2025</p>
</footer>
```
